content: LLM numeric scoring (0-10) is poorly calibrated by default: scores cluster (central tendency or leniency bias), vary across model versions and sampling, and raw 1-10 prompts sacrifice reliability. Best practice: decompose into rubric criteria with anchored descriptions per level, score each criterion (or use pass/fail sub-judgments) then aggregate to the 0-10 in code; calibrate against a golden set of human-expert-scored conversations; measure inter-rater agreement (e.g., correlation/kappa) between judge and expert; re-validate on model upgrades. Legal-domain studies show rubric-guided LLM judges can reach high expert agreement when rubrics come from domain experts.
